away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Alysha Clark
players.injury_comment: Clark has missed the past two games due to a left leg injury, but there's a chance she could get back out there Monday evening. Clark has been an inconsistent part of the Wings' rotation this season.

players.name: Alysha Clark
players.injury_comment: Clark has missed the past two games due to a left leg injury, but there's a chance she could get back out there Monday evening. Clark has been an inconsistent part of the Wings' rotation this season.

players.name: Odyssey Sims
players.injury_comment: Sims hasn't played since the 104-96 loss to the Sparks on June 5. While Sims remains sidelined, Aziaha James should continue to see more minutes.

players.name: Alanna Smith
players.injury_comment: Smith suffered the face injury during Saturday's win over the Sky, and now she will miss Monday's game, which might open up minutes for Maddy Siegrist and Awak Kuier. For now, Smith should be considered questionable for Thursday's game in Las Vegas.

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Jade Melbourne
players.injury_comment: With Jordan Horston (foot) sidelined, Melbourne started in Seattle's last two regular-season games. However, she tweaked her ankle in the 93-73 loss to Phoenix on Saturday, ending her night after only 11 minutes. If Melbourne can't suit up Monday, Katie Lou Samuelson could find herself in the starting lineup.

players.name: Jade Melbourne
players.injury_comment: With Jordan Horston (foot) sidelined, Melbourne started in Seattle's last two regular-season games. However, she tweaked her ankle in the 93-73 loss to Phoenix on Saturday, ending her night after only 11 minutes. If Melbourne can't suit up Monday, Katie Lou Samuelson could find herself in the starting lineup.

players.name: Jordan Horston
players.injury_comment: Horston will miss her third consecutive game Monday, and for now, she should be considered questionable for Thursday's matchup with the Liberty. Jade Melbourne (foot), who had been starting in Horston's absence, is questionable for Monday's game, and if she can't give it a go, Katie Lou Samuelson would have an opportunity to start.

players.name: Ezi Magbegor
players.injury_comment: Magbegor sustained her injury while playing for Australia in the 2026 FIBA Basketball World Cup Qualifiers, and she appears to be on track to miss the start of the WNBA regular season as a result. The 26-year-old has played a prominent role in Seattle's frontcourt across the past several seasons, most recently averaging 8.0 points, 6.2 rebounds, 2.2 blocks and 2.1 assists in 27.3 minutes per game while starting every regular-season matchup in 2025, while her absence to begin the 2026 season could lead to increased opportunities for Dominique Malonga, Stefanie Dolson and rookie Awa Fam.

players.name: Taina Mair
players.injury_comment: Mair has logged only five minutes across two regular-season contests for the Storm during the 2026 campaign. She is signed to a developmental deal, limiting her to 12 regular-season appearances.
